channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: MMDA to Enforce Penalties on Littering Using NCAP Cameras
item.description: The Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) is set to strictly implement penalties on individuals who litter in public spaces, using the No Contact Apprehension Policy (NCAP) cameras as part ...

item.title: Canadians deported from Gaza flotilla in Israel now in Turkey amid reports of mistreatment
item.description: Twelve Canadians who participated in the Global Sumud Flotilla attempting to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza were detained by Israeli authorities and subsequently deported to Turkey. Foreign Affairs ...

item.title: US Bars Tanzanian Police Commander Following Alleged Human Rights Violations
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The United States has officially barred Faustine Jackson Mafwele, Senior Assistant Commissioner of the Tanzania Police Force, from entering the US. This action follows credible reports...

item.title: Agusan del Sur Prepares Free Transport, Tours, and Cultural Village for Palarong Pambansa 2026
item.description: The 66th Palarong Pambansa, scheduled from May 24 to May 31, 2026, will be hosted by Agusan del Sur, welcoming thousands of student-athletes, delegates, and spectators. In a bid to enhance the experie...

item.title: Invasive Australian Red Claw Crayfish Threaten Southern African Rivers and Fisheries
item.description: Highly invasive Australian red claw crayfish (Cherax quadricarinatus) are spreading rapidly across southern Africa, threatening local fish populations, aquatic biodiversity, and the livelihoods of sma...

item.title: Sindh Government Declares Four-Day Holiday for Eid ul Adha from May 26 to 29
item.description: The Sindh government has officially declared a four-day holiday on the occasion of Eid ul Adha, which will be celebrated on May 27, 2026. According to the government notification, all provincial gover...

item.title: Proposed Kenyan Excise Duty May Increase Costs for Tanzanian Glass Exports
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Kioo Limited, the largest glass manufacturer in East and Central Africa, has expressed concern over Kenya's proposed 35 per cent excise duty on glass bottles imported from East African...

item.title: Pep Guardiola set to depart Manchester City after ten successful years
item.description: Pep Guardiola has announced that he will be leaving Manchester City at the conclusion of the 2025-26 season, ending a remarkable ten-year tenure at the Etihad Stadium. Since taking charge in 2016, the...

item.title: Fatal Collision in Scarborough Claims One Life and Injures Three
item.description: On Thursday evening in Scarborough, a serious head-on collision resulted in one fatality and three serious injuries. Toronto police reported that the incident occurred around 10 p.m. at the intersecti...

item.title: Michael Carrick Appointed Permanent Manchester United Manager After Interim Success
item.description: Manchester United don officially appoint Michael Carrick as their permanent manager, signing am for two years after five months wey e dey serve as interim boss. Carrick take over after the club sack R...

item.title: Photographer Jesse Echevarria Captures the Striking Landscapes of Chile's Atacama Desert
item.description: Photographer Jesse Echevarria recently shared a compelling series of images showcasing the Atacama Desert, one of the driest and most remote regions on Earth. Located in a narrow strip along the Pacif...

item.title: Siyakholwa Kuse’s Journey from Addiction to WBC World Champion
item.description: Siyakholwa Kuse, once struggling with severe drug addiction and malnutrition, has made a remarkable comeback to boxing, largely thanks to the intervention and guidance of Mlandeli Tengimfene. In 2022,...

item.title: Rising Tick Populations and Tick-Borne Diseases Across Canada
item.description: Experts are reporting a notable increase in tick populations and tick-borne illnesses across Canada, driven largely by warmer winters and climate change. Blacklegged ticks, also known as deer ticks, a...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over President Trump Sitting During Oval Office Event
item.description: During a recent Oval Office event, President Donald Trump was observed remaining seated while making remarks, prompting discussion about his public appearances and changing behavior in his second term...

item.title: Senator Solomon Adeola Emerges APC Governorship Candidate for Ogun State 2027
item.description: For Ogun State, APC don declare Senator Solomon Adeola, wey dey represent Ogun West, as winner of their governorship primary wey hold across all 20 local government areas on Thursday. According to Wal...

item.title: Understanding IELTS: Why Filipino Students and Workers Must Take It for Opportunities Abroad
item.description: The International English Language Testing System (IELTS) is an English proficiency exam widely recognized around the world. Many Filipinos encounter it when planning to study, work, or migrate abroad...

item.title: Lawyer Njeri Maina Refutes Violence Claims During Gachagua Impeachment
item.description: During a tense courtroom session on the impeachment of former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua, lawyer Jane Njeri Maina firmly defended her actions and dismissed allegations that she was involved in ...

item.title: Review: Stunning Visuals Overshadow Thin Story in 'The Mandalorian and Grogu'
item.description: ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ offers a visually striking experience, but it struggles to deliver a compelling narrative. Pedro Pascal reprises his role from the TV series, embarking on a mission to secu...

item.title: Pakistan's CDF Asim Munir undertakes official visit to Iran for peace talks
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Chief of the Army Staff Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir has departed for an official visit to Iran on Friday, according to security sources. During his visit, CDF Munir...

item.title: Guidelines for Using AI Effectively in Education
item.description: The use of AI in education often sparks debate among students and institutions. While some schools adopt a strict 'AI is bad' stance, many students find AI to be a valuable tool when used responsibly....
